gold.rationale: 公开可核对的信息仅能支持“按通报完成约7小时出舱并安装空间碎片防护装置、人员分工与返回舱段”等事实；文本将这些事实进一步解读为“疑似碎片击中后的临时修补、险情与隐瞒”，属于把不确定猜测包装成主要叙事结论。配图中两张关键“疑点图”经裁剪、对比度/锐化与红圈箭头标注后被当作证据使用，但其所指向的“裂痕/碎屑/擦伤”无法从原始公开通报中得到验证，且与呈现“作业顺利、状态稳定”的画面在语义与证据指向上不匹配，整体误导性大于真实成分，因此判为 Mostly False。

atoms: Close-up view of an astronaut's gloved hand in a white spacesuit reaching towards a metallic structure.
The astronaut is wearing a white glove with a textured, dark grey grip pattern on the fingers.
A thick orange cable or hose is visible in the lower left foreground.
A white robotic arm or mechanical structure is visible in the upper left.
The background is black space with indistinct structural elements.
The image has a slight blueish tint and pixelation, suggesting a screen capture or low-resolution video frame.
A wide shot of an astronaut in a white spacesuit working on the exterior of a space station module.
A large white robotic arm is positioned near the astronaut.
A red circle overlay highlights a small dark spot on the white robotic arm.
A red rectangular box with a zoomed-in inset highlights a small dark spot on the astronaut's white glove.
Red arrows connect the red circle and the red box to the inset.
Chinese text annotations in red point to the spots, reading '疑似擦痕/碎屑 (网友圈出)' which translates to 'Suspected scratch/debris (circled by netizens)'.
Watermark text '广西日报' (Guangxi Daily) is visible at the bottom right.
Text '舱外航天服图像b' (Extravehicular spacesuit image b) is visible at the bottom left.
A split-screen image comparing a 'Original Frame' (left) with a zoomed-in, annotated version (right).
The left side shows a smaller, clearer frame labeled '原画面' (Original Frame) with an astronaut and part of a space station.
The right side shows a heavily zoomed-in, low-resolution crop of the astronaut's suit.
Red hand-drawn lines and arrows outline a specific area on the astronaut's suit in the right panel.
Large white text at the top reads '03-16 19:xx 截图' (03-16 19:xx Screenshot).
A watermark '@人民日报' (People's Daily) is visible in the bottom left of the original frame.
A schematic or diagram showing Earth surrounded by a dense ring of white dots representing space debris or satellites.
The Earth is depicted as a blue and white sphere in the center.
The background is black space.
The image appears to be a stock illustration or graphic representation rather than a photograph.
A wide shot of an astronaut in a white spacesuit floating outside a large, curved space station module.
A robotic arm is visible in the upper left, interacting with the astronaut or equipment.
The background is the blackness of space.
A blue and white logo with Chinese characters '新华社' (Xinhua News Agency) is in the bottom right corner.
The lighting is bright, creating high contrast on the white suit.
A horizontal split image showing two different views of an astronaut in a white spacesuit.
The top half shows a close-up of the astronaut's torso and legs against a black background.
The bottom half shows a wider view of the astronaut floating near the space station with Earth visible in the background.
The astronaut is holding onto a mechanical arm or structure in both views.
The Earth appears as a blue curve with white clouds in the bottom view.

retrieved_evidence.text: 有关神舟二十一号乘组出舱活动的讨论中，有描述提到空间站舱体外壁、设备边缘等位置存在细微擦痕或疑似碎屑痕迹。根据航天工程人员介绍，长期在轨飞行的空间站外表面不可避免会受到微流星体、空间环境和频繁舱外作业的综合影响，因此在设备舱段和外壁结构上出现局部磨损或沉积物属于工程设计考虑范围内的情况。相关技术团队会通过在轨巡检、地面数据分析和后续维护安排，对这些细节进行系统评估与记录。

【#神21乘组圆满完成第二次出舱活动#】3月16日19时35分，经过7小时的出舱活动，神舟二十一号乘组航天员张陆、武飞、张洪章密切协同，在空间站机械臂和地面科研人员的配合支持下，圆满完成空间碎片防护装置安装等任务。出舱航天员张陆、武飞已安全返回问天实验舱，出舱活动取得圆满成功。

至此，张陆已 ​​​
【神舟二十一号航天员乘组圆满完成第二次出舱活动】据中国载人航天工程办公室消息，北京时间3月16日19时35分，经过7小时的出舱活动，神舟二十一号乘组航天员张陆、武飞、张洪章密切协同，在空间站机械臂和地面科研人员的配合支持下，圆满完成空间碎片防护装置安装等任务。出舱航天员张陆、武飞已安全返 ​​​
【#神21乘组完成第二次出舱#】#航天员张陆已出舱活动6次# ​​​​据中国载人航天工程办公室消息，北京时间2026年3月16日19时35分，经过7小时的出舱活动，神舟二十一号乘组航天员张陆、武飞、张洪章密切协同，在空间站机械臂和地面科研人员的配合支持下，圆满完成空间碎片防护装置安装等任务。出舱航天 ​​​
多家媒体近日从中国载人航天工程办公室获悉，神舟二十一号乘组在第二次出舱活动中，重点执行了空间碎片防护装置安装等多项在轨任务。参与任务的技术专家介绍，此类装置旨在提升空间站对微小碎片的防护能力，出舱航天员需在轨完成支架固定、组件连接和外观检查等工序。除防护装置相关操作外，任务还包括对部分舱外设施的例行巡检和状态记录。
在对神舟二十一号出舱过程的技术分析中，一些讨论聚焦于同一舱段局部区域在不同视角和放大比例下呈现的细节差异，试图解读设备表面的划痕、遮挡物或阴影变化。航天领域专家指出，空间站外壁和设备表面通常覆盖有多层防护材料、光学传感器和电缆束，受光照角度、反射条件及航天员作业姿态影响，局部纹理和亮暗变化较为复杂，需要结合在轨工程数据、材料特性和标准工况，才能对所谓前后变化或异常作出科学判断。
中国空间站配备的大型机械臂是支持神舟二十一号等任务开展舱外作业的关键设备之一。公开资料显示，该机械臂具备多自由度关节，可在核心舱与实验舱之间转运货物、搬运大型舱外设备，并辅助航天员在轨作业和位姿调整。根据航天工程部门的介绍，机械臂在任务过程中会根据预定程序和实时指令进行频繁姿态调整，以适应不同航程和负载需求，同时还需配合空间站整体姿态控制系统，保持组合体结构安全和动力学稳定。
多家媒体本周集中转发中国载人航天工程办公室发布的消息称，神舟二十一号乘组第二次出舱活动在空间站轨道段顺利实施。通报以简要方式介绍了出舱时间、参加人员、主要作业内容及任务完成情况，强调天地协同配合和地面科研团队的技术支持作用。多家报道援引有关人士观点认为，本次舱外作业进一步检验了空间站相关系统的在轨工作能力，对后续长期运行具有示范意义。
多家媒体近日援引中国载人航天工程办公室消息报道，正在执行长期在轨飞行任务的神舟二十一号乘组本周展开了本次飞行阶段的第二次出舱活动。相关报道指出，此次出舱是围绕空间站在轨维护与升级安排实施的一项既定任务，旨在在轨检验多项设备性能，并为后续航天活动积累经验。有关方面称，此次任务在既定轨道条件下组织实施，体现了我国空间站运营管理的常态化水平。
据多家媒体转发的中国载人航天工程办公室通报，历时约7小时的神舟二十一号乘组第二次出舱活动圆满完成，期间既定任务目标基本实现。出舱航天员张陆、武飞已安全返回问天实验舱，完成舱门关合、密封性检查及舱内环境恢复等后续程序。据介绍，地面科研和工程团队正在对任务过程中采集的图像数据和设备运行参数进行整理分析，为后续在轨工作提供参考。
据多家媒体援引中国载人航天工程办公室介绍，在神舟二十一号乘组第二次出舱活动中，执行舱外作业的为航天员张陆、武飞，两人通过舱门依次出舱，利用空间站机械臂和自身移动能力开展多点位操作。舱内航天员张洪章则负责配合出舱流程，与地面科研人员保持稳定通信支持，对舱体状态和仪表参数进行监视。报道指出，天地协同在关键步骤中多次进行口令核对和状态确认。
据多家媒体转引中国载人航天工程办公室通报，神舟二十一号乘组第二次出舱活动按计划于北京时间2026年3月16日19时35分开始实施。报道援引任务一线人员介绍称，在地面系统综合评估空间站姿态、轨道环境及各项设备状态后，指令于当晚下达，乘组成员随即按程序完成舱外作业相关准备。相关细节仍在进一步梳理当中。
多家媒体根据中国载人航天工程办公室发布的信息报道，神舟二十一号乘组本次出舱活动总时长约7小时。任务期间，出舱航天员围绕舱外工作区域逐项执行既定操作，包括设备转运、装调以及状态复核等环节，地面控制中心通过测控系统对关键节点进行实时监测和调度。据参与任务的技术人员介绍，此次出舱作业节奏总体平稳，各项技术指标基本符合预期。
神舟二十一号乘组在轨期间还承担了多项科学实验平台的集成与调试工作，包括材料科学、生物医药和空间物理等领域。根据相关研究团队的公开介绍，这些实验装置多数安装在空间站实验舱内，但部分需要通过舱外安装平台与舱外暴露载荷接口对接，以直接暴露于太空环境下运行。航天员在舱外作业中需要完成机械连接、电气插拔和状态确认等操作，确保实验装置在轨稳定工作并按计划采集科学数据。
空间碎片风险一直是中国空间站和神舟二十一号乘组在轨安全评估中的重要因素之一。根据科普资料，地球低轨道区域分布着大量报废卫星、火箭残骸及微小碎片，飞行速度高、碰撞能量大，可能对在轨航天器构成威胁。因此，空间站设计采用了多层防护板、被动防护装置以及必要时的规避机动方案，同时在舱外作业任务规划中预留对防护装置进行检查、加装或加固的步骤，以提升整体抗碎片能力，降低轨道碎片撞击风险。
在空间站运营阶段，中国航天科技集团和航天员科研训练中心等机构承担神舟二十一号等任务的总体技术管理与飞行控制工作。根据近年来公开的任务新闻，这些机构在每次出舱活动前需进行多轮地面模拟演练，包括对外部结构、关键设备及潜在风险源的梳理，明确航天员行走路径和作业顺序。任务执行过程中，地面指挥团队会依据遥测数据和航天员口头反馈，适时对作业步骤进行优化，确保舱外活动与空间站关键设备运行互不干扰。
神舟二十一号任务所在的中国空间站长期在轨运行，需要复杂的电源、热控和通信系统保障。太阳翼阵列在各舱段展开，为组合体提供主要电力来源，其方位角和俯仰角会随轨道位置与太阳高度自动调整。站内外布设的大量天线和数据链路维系与地面测控站的实时通信，使航天员在执行舱外作业、设备维护和科学实验过程中能够随时接受指令、回传状态信息，并在必要时得到地面专家的技术支持。
神舟二十一号任务被官方描述为圆满完成，中国空间站组合体继续在轨稳定运行。根据公开资料，执行该任务的乘组在轨期间按计划进行了多次舱外作业，航天员身着新一代舱外航天服，通过气闸舱进出核心舱外，完成设备安装、检测和维护等工作。相关报道多以正规新闻宣传风格呈现航天员在轨作业的标准场景，强调任务组织周密、技术状态可靠以及中国载人航天工程体系化管理的成熟度。

text_analysis.alignment_reasoning: The post accurately reports the core facts from the official evidence but introduces significant speculative narratives regarding 'emergency repairs' and 'hidden injuries' that are not supported by the official ground truth.
text_analysis.t2_evidence: The post speculates that the mission was a 'temporary patch' or 'emergency disposal' due to suspected damage, whereas the evidence states it was a 'routine inspection and status recording' and 'planned' task.
t2_problematic_subclaims: Claim that the installation was a 'temporary patch' or 'emergency disposal' instead of a routine task
Speculation that astronauts had 'minor scratches' or 'surface wear' not disclosed in official reports
Interpretation of 'safe return' as evidence of a 'nervewracking moment' or 'sudden incident'
Narrative that the mission was a 'forced early conclusion' due to communication or thermal control issues
text_analysis.global_assessment: The post accurately summarizes the official timeline and personnel but distorts the nature of the mission by framing a routine task as a potential emergency repair and inventing unverified details about injuries and technical glitches.

images.explanation: A clean, unedited news photo showing an astronaut during a spacewalk, matching the official footage described in the evidence.

image_analysis.global_notes: The post discusses the Shenzhou 21 spacewalk and the controversy surrounding it. The images provided are a mix of official footage (clean photos), netizen-edited screenshots (photos with red circles/overlays), and a schematic illustration of space debris.

judge.level3_rationale: 本文虽然引用了真实的出舱画面和网友标注的疑似痕迹，但核心叙事将一次常规的防护装置安装任务歪曲为‘紧急修补’和‘隐瞒伤情’，与官方通报的‘圆满完成’事实相悖，且使用了示意图作为佐证，导致整体结论严重误导。
judge.reasoning_trace: The post's core claim contradicts official evidence (T2), while the images show real netizen annotations (V2) and a schematic (V1) used to support a false narrative (C2). The combination of a fabricated core claim with real but misleading visual evidence results in a Mostly False verdict.
judge.core_claim: The post claims that the official report on the Shenzhou-21 EVA was misleading, suggesting the task was an 'emergency patch' for suspected damage, and that the images show 'suspected scratches' or debris highlighted by netizens.
key_subclaims: The task was an 'emergency patch' or 'temporary disposal' rather than a routine installation.
The official report hides details about 'minor scratches' or 'surface wear' on the astronauts' suits.
The images provided show specific 'suspected scratches' or debris highlighted by netizens.
The official report is misleading by using vague terms like 'completed successfully'.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The task was an 'emergency patch' or 'temporary disposal' rather than a routine installation.
subclaim_findings.note: Official reports state the task was to 'install space debris protection devices' and 'inspect status', which is a routine maintenance task, not an emergency patch.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The official report hides details about 'minor scratches' or 'surface wear' on the astronauts' suits.
subclaim_findings.note: The official report explicitly states the astronauts 'completed the task successfully' and 'safely returned'. There is no evidence of hidden injuries or undisclosed scratches.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The images provided show specific 'suspected scratches' or debris highlighted by netizens.
subclaim_findings.note: Image 2 clearly shows red circles and text annotations ('Suspected scratch/debris') overlaid on the astronaut's suit and robotic arm, matching the post's description of netizen annotations.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The official report is misleading by using vague terms like 'completed successfully'.
subclaim_findings.note: The report is brief and standard for such missions, but the post's claim that this brevity implies hidden danger is a subjective interpretation rather than a factual contradiction.

judge.verdict_reasoning: The post fabricates a narrative that the mission was an 'emergency patch' and hides injuries, which contradicts the official evidence stating it was a routine task completed successfully. While the images of 'suspected scratches' are real (V2), they are used to support a false conclusion. The inclusion of a schematic illustration (V1) and the misleading context of the text (T2) combined with the contradictory core claim pushes the verdict to Mostly False.

image_findings.explanation: A standard close-up of an astronaut's hand near a mechanical structure, consistent with the official EVA footage.
The image contains clear visual editing (V2) in the form of red circles, arrows, and text overlays ('Suspected scratch/debris') added by netizens, which the post uses to support its narrative.
The image is a composite (V2) showing an 'original frame' and a zoomed-in crop with red hand-drawn annotations, matching the post's description of how images are processed.
The image is a schematic illustration (V1) of Earth surrounded by debris, used to represent the context of space debris protection rather than a real photo.
A standard press photo of an astronaut working on the space station, consistent with official reports.
A standard broadcast still of an astronaut working on the space station with Earth in the background.
